 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-i6c","title":"Custom Dashboard Builder","description":"## Background

As the Flywheel Gateway platform matures, users need the ability to create personalized views of their agent ecosystem, costs, and activity metrics. A custom dashboard builder enables users to construct tailored monitoring interfaces without requiring engineering intervention, democratizing data visualization across the organization.

## Problem Statement

Currently, users are limited to predefined dashboard layouts that may not align with their specific monitoring needs. Different roles (developers, managers, finance) have distinct requirements for data visualization. Without customization capabilities, users either lack visibility into relevant metrics or must request engineering resources for custom reports.

## Technical Approach

### Core Architecture

The dashboard builder will leverage react-grid-layout for drag-and-drop widget placement, providing a familiar interface similar to tools like Grafana or Datadog dashboards.

```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/analytics/DashboardBuilder.tsx
interface Dashboard {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description?: string;
  ownerId: string;
  visibility: 'private' | 'team' | 'public';
  teamId?: string;
  layout: DashboardLayout;
  widgets: Widget[];
  refreshInterval: number; // seconds, 0 = manual only
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}

interface DashboardLayout {
  columns: number; // typically 12 or 24
  rowHeight: number;
  margin: [number, number];
  containerPadding: [number, number];
}

interface Widget {
  id: string;
  type: WidgetType;
  title: string;
  position: { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number };
  config: WidgetConfig;
  dataSource: DataSourceConfig;
}

type WidgetType = 
  | 'metric-card'      // Single KPI with trend
  | 'line-chart'       // Time series
  | 'bar-chart'        // Categorical comparison
  | 'pie-chart'        // Distribution
  | 'table'            // Tabular data
  | 'agent-list'       // Agent status list
  | 'activity-feed'    // Recent events stream
  | 'cost-breakdown'   // Cost visualization
  | 'heatmap'          // Usage patterns
  | 'gauge'            // Progress/capacity
  | 'text'             // Markdown content
  | 'iframe';          // External embeds
```

### Widget Type Specifications

**Metric Cards:**
- Single value display with optional sparkline
- Trend indicator (up/down/neutral with percentage)
- Threshold-based coloring (green/yellow/red)
- Click-through to detailed view

**Charts (Line, Bar, Pie):**
- Built on Chart.js or Recharts
- Configurable time ranges
- Multiple series support
- Legend positioning
- Tooltip customization
- Export as PNG/SVG

**Tables:**
- Sortable columns
- Pagination
- Column visibility toggles
- Row click actions
- Inline search/filter
- CSV export

**Agent Lists:**
- Status indicators (active/idle/error/offline)
- Quick actions (pause, configure, view logs)
- Grouping by team/project/model
- Search and filter

**Activity Feeds:**
- Real-time updates via WebSocket
- Event type filtering
- Expandable details
- Jump to related entity

### Widget Configuration Panel

```typescript
interface WidgetConfigPanel {
  // Common settings
  title: string;
  description?: string;
  refreshInterval?: number;
  
  // Data source
  dataSource: {
    type: 'api' | 'query' | 'static';
    endpoint?: string;
    query?: string; // For custom queries
    filters?: Record<string, any>;
    timeRange?: TimeRange;
  };
  
  // Display settings (type-specific)
  display: {
    colorScheme?: string;
    showLegend?: boolean;
    showGrid?: boolean;
    // ... type-specific options
  };
  
  // Thresholds
  thresholds?: {
    warning?: number;
    critical?: number;
  };
}
```

### Auto-Refresh Configuration

```typescript
interface RefreshConfig {
  globalInterval: number; // Dashboard-wide default
  widgetOverrides: Map<string, number>; // Per-widget overrides
  pauseOnHidden: boolean; // Pause when tab not visible
  pauseOnError: boolean; // Pause widget on consecutive errors
  maxRetries: number; // Before pausing
}
```

### Sharing and Access Control

```typescript
interface DashboardSharing {
  visibility: 'private' | 'team' | 'public';
  teamId?: string; // Required for 'team' visibility
  
  // Granular permissions
  viewers: string[]; // User IDs with read access
  editors: string[]; // User IDs with edit access
  
  // Public options
  publicSlug?: string; // URL-friendly identifier
  requireAuth: boolean; // Require login even for public
  embedEnabled: boolean; // Allow iframe embedding
  embedToken?: string; // For secure embedding
}
```

### Backend Storage

```sql
-- Dashboards table
CREATE TABLE dashboards (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  owner_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  visibility VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'private',
  team_id UUID REFERENCES teams(id),
  layout JSONB NOT NULL,
  widgets JSONB NOT NULL,
  refresh_interval INTEGER DEFAULT 60,
  public_slug VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
  embed_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  embed_token VARCHAR(64),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Dashboard permissions
CREATE TABLE dashboard_permissions (
  dashboard_id UUID REFERENCES dashboards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  permission VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL, -- 'view' or 'edit'
  PRIMARY KEY (dashboard_id, user_id)
);

-- Dashboard favorites for quick access
CREATE TABLE dashboard_favorites (
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  dashboard_id UUID REFERENCES dashboards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  PRIMARY KEY (user_id, dashboard_id)
);
```

### API Endpoints

```typescript
// Dashboard CRUD
GET    /api/v1/dashboards                    // List user's dashboards
POST   /api/v1/dashboards                    // Create dashboard
GET    /api/v1/dashboards/:id                // Get dashboard
PUT    /api/v1/dashboards/:id                // Update dashboard
DELETE /api/v1/dashboards/:id                // Delete dashboard
POST   /api/v1/dashboards/:id/duplicate      // Clone dashboard

// Sharing
PUT    /api/v1/dashboards/:id/sharing        // Update sharing settings
GET    /api/v1/dashboards/:id/permissions    // List permissions
POST   /api/v1/dashboards/:id/permissions    // Add permission
DELETE /api/v1/dashboards/:id/permissions/:userId

// Widget data
GET    /api/v1/dashboards/:id/widgets/:widgetId/data  // Fetch widget data

// Public access
GET    /api/v1/public/dashboards/:slug       // Public dashboard view
GET    /api/v1/embed/:token                  // Embedded dashboard
```

## File Locations

- apps/web/src/components/analytics/DashboardBuilder.tsx - Main builder component
- apps/web/src/components/analytics/DashboardGrid.tsx - react-grid-layout wrapper
- apps/web/src/components/analytics/widgets/ - Widget components directory
- apps/web/src/components/analytics/WidgetConfigPanel.tsx - Configuration sidebar
- apps/web/src/hooks/useDashboard.ts - Dashboard state management
- apps/gateway/src/services/dashboard.service.ts - Backend service
- apps/gateway/src/controllers/dashboard.controller.ts - API controller
- packages/shared/src/types/dashboard.ts - Shared type definitions

## Dependencies

- react-grid-layout: ^1.4.0 (drag-and-drop grid)
- recharts: ^2.8.0 (charting library)
- @tanstack/react-query: For data fetching and caching
- date-fns: Time range calculations
- react-markdown: For text widgets

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Users can create, edit, and delete custom dashboards
2. Drag-and-drop widget placement works smoothly on desktop browsers
3. All specified widget types are implemented and functional
4. Widget configuration panel allows customizing all relevant settings
5. Auto-refresh works with configurable intervals (15s, 30s, 1m, 5m, 15m, off)
6. Dashboards can be shared with teams or made public
7. Public dashboards are accessible via slug URLs
8. Dashboard state persists correctly across sessions
9. Performance: Dashboard with 20 widgets loads in < 3 seconds
10. Mobile-responsive layout degrades gracefully
11. Widgets display loading states and error handling
12. Export functionality available for charts and tables

## Testing Strategy

- Unit tests for widget components
- Integration tests for dashboard CRUD operations
- E2E tests for drag-and-drop interactions
- Performance tests for large dashboards
- Cross-browser testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

## Reference

PLAN.md section 21.8 - Custom Dashboard Builder

Reference: `flywheel_gateway-d8b`.

- [ ] Dashboard layout serialization/deserialization is stable and backward-compatible within a major version
- [ ] Widget registry rejects invalid configs with actionable errors
- [ ] Grid/layout constraints prevent overlapping/invalid placements

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Create/update/delete dashboard via REST and verify persistence + retrieval

### E2E Tests (UI)
- [ ] Drag/drop widgets, resize, save, reload → layout persists and renders correctly

### Logging
- [ ] Logs include correlationId + dashboardId + widgetCount + action; widget payloads are validated and sanitized

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-jp1","title":"EPIC: Phase 2 - Core Features","description":"## Overview
Phase 2 adds multi-agent coordination capabilities, state management, and account management for production use.

## Phase 2 Goal
Multi-agent coordination and state management

## Key Deliverables

### Additional Agent Drivers
- ACP Agent Driver (Agent Client Protocol)
  - JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio
  - IDE integration compatibility
  - Protocol adapter for different agents

### Agent Communication
- Agent Mail integration (MCP client)
  - Messaging between agents
  - Thread management
  - Inbox/outbox operations
- File reservation system
  - Advisory locks with TTL
  - Exclusive/shared modes
  - Reservation map UI component

### Conflict Management
- Conflict detection baseline
  - File conflict detection
  - WebSocket events for conflicts
  - Alert generation

### State Management
- Checkpoint/restore system
  - Delta-based progressive checkpointing
  - Full checkpoint every 5th
  - Compression with zstd
  - Bounded restore time
- Auto-Healing Context Window Management
  - Graduated thresholds (warning 75%, critical 85%, emergency 95%)
  - Proactive summarization
  - Seamless agent rotation with context transfer
- Context pack builder
  - Token budgeting
  - Source prioritization
  - Overflow handling

### Operations
- flywheel_gateway-76h: Supervisor & Daemon Management [P2]
- Job orchestration for long-running operations
  - Context builds, scans, exports
  - Progress events via WebSocket
  - Job status endpoints
- History tracking system
  - Prompt/response summaries
  - Token usage tracking
  - Outcome recording

### Reliability
- Idempotency middleware
  - Idempotency key header
  - Response caching
  - Duplicate request handling

### Account Management
- CAAM integration (BYOA + BYOK)
  - Profile vault management
  - Account rotation on limits
  - Usage tracking per profile
  - Health monitoring

## Phase Completion Criteria
- [ ] Multiple agents can coordinate via Agent Mail
- [ ] File reservations prevent edit conflicts
- [ ] Conflicts detected and alert generated
- [ ] Checkpoints created automatically with deltas
- [ ] Restore from checkpoint works within 200ms
- [ ] Context window auto-heals before hitting limit
- [ ] Long-running operations show progress
- [ ] BYOA accounts rotate on rate limit
- [ ] All operations are idempotent

## Testing Requirements
- Unit test coverage >80% for all new services
- Integration tests for Agent Mail operations
- Integration tests for checkpoint/restore cycle
- E2E tests for multi-agent coordination scenarios
- Performance tests for checkpoint restoration time
- Load tests for context window management
- Structured test logging + artifact capture per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`

### Logging
- [ ] Unit/integration tests emit structured logs with `correlationId` + relevant entity IDs (never secrets)
- [ ] E2E tests capture artifacts (trace/video/screenshot/console logs) and surface failure context per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`


## Success Criteria

- [ ] Multi-agent coordination works end-to-end: Agent Mail integration + reservations + conflict alerts + checkpoints + context packs
- [ ] Auto-healing context window management demonstrated (thresholds, compaction/rotation, safe handoff)
- [ ] Long-running operations return job IDs + progress events; jobs are observable and cancelable
- [ ] Idempotency middleware prevents duplicated side effects for mutating endpoints
- [ ] BYOA-gated execution is enforced (no account → actionable error with recovery hints)
- [ ] Tests: unit + integration + E2E coverage for new UX flows and failure modes per `flywheel_gateway-d8b`

## Reference

- `flywheel_gateway-y19` (embedded `docs/PLAN.md` spec snapshot)

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-kue","title":"TASK: Risk Mitigations and Resilience","description":"## Overview

This bead tracks the mitigation strategies for the 16+ risks identified in PLAN.md §26 (Risk Register). While many mitigations are implemented by specific feature beads, this bead ensures all risks have explicit mitigation coverage and testing.

## Background & Reasoning

### Why This Bead Exists
Risk management requires visibility. Individual features implement mitigations, but without central tracking we can't verify:
1. All risks have assigned mitigations
2. Mitigations are actually tested
3. Residual risk is acceptable

### Risk Matrix

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Primary Mitigation Bead |
|------|------------|--------|-------------------------|
| Agent runaway | Medium | High | flywheel_gateway-398 (Lifecycle) |
| Account quota exhaustion | High | High | flywheel_gateway-41h (CAAM) |
| File conflicts | Medium | Medium | flywheel_gateway-5nm + flywheel_gateway-3b1 |
| Context overflow | High | Medium | flywheel_gateway-ew1 (Auto-Healing) |
| Work handoff failures | Medium | Medium | flywheel_gateway-2pl (Handoff Protocol) |
| Checkpoint storage explosion | Medium | Medium | flywheel_gateway-36m (Delta Checkpoints) |
| Network interruption | Medium | Medium | flywheel_gateway-46c (WebSocket) |
| Daemon failure | Low | High | flywheel_gateway-76h (Supervisor) |
| Data loss | Low | Critical | flywheel_gateway-36m + flywheel_gateway-toe |
| Security breach | Low | Critical | flywheel_gateway-bz1 (Security) |
| Coordination visibility | Medium | Medium | flywheel_gateway-c6q (Collab Graph) |
| Performance degradation | Medium | Medium | flywheel_gateway-mag (Performance) |
| Provider outage | Low | High | flywheel_gateway-41h (Multi-provider) |
| Cost overruns | Medium | High | flywheel_gateway-rgx (Cost Analytics) |
| Notification fatigue | Medium | Low | flywheel_gateway-59c (Notifications) |
| Agent performance blind spots | Medium | Medium | flywheel_gateway-6wp (Performance Analytics) |
| Flywheel visibility gaps | Low | Medium | flywheel_gateway-66n (Velocity Dashboard) |

## Risk Mitigation Details

### 1. Agent Runaway (Medium/High)
**Symptoms**: Agent consumes excessive tokens, runs indefinitely, or produces harmful output.
**Mitigations**:
- Token limits per agent session (configurable, default 100K)
- Activity detection: no progress for N minutes triggers alert
- Auto-interrupt after configurable timeout
- Checkpoint before long operations
- DCG blocks destructive commands

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Agent exceeding token limit is terminated gracefully
- [ ] Inactive agent triggers alert after 5 minutes
- [ ] Auto-interrupt stops runaway agent
- [ ] Checkpoint created before risky operations

### 2. Account Quota Exhaustion (High/High)
**Symptoms**: All API keys hit rate limits, agents blocked.
**Mitigations**:
- CAAM pool rotation across multiple accounts
- Per-account cooldown tracking
- Multi-provider support (Claude + Codex + Gemini)
- Graceful queuing when all accounts exhausted

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Rotation occurs automatically on rate limit
- [ ] Cooldown prevents immediate retry on same account
- [ ] Fallback to alternate provider works
- [ ] Queue drains when account recovers

### 3. File Conflicts (Medium/Medium)
**Symptoms**: Multiple agents edit same file, causing overwrites.
**Mitigations**:
- Advisory file reservations with TTL
- Conflict detection alerts
- Intelligent Conflict Resolution Assistant suggests merges

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Reservation prevents second agent from editing
- [ ] Conflict detected when reservation bypassed
- [ ] AI suggestions generate reasonable merges
- [ ] Conflict history preserved for audit

### 4. Context Overflow (High/Medium)
**Symptoms**: Agent context window fills up, loses coherence.
**Mitigations**:
- Auto-Healing Context Windows with graduated thresholds (75%/85%/95%)
- Proactive summarization at warning threshold
- Seamless agent rotation with context transfer
- Context pack builder with token budgeting

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Warning at 75% utilization
- [ ] Auto-summarization at 85%
- [ ] Rotation at 95% preserves context
- [ ] No data loss during rotation

### 5. Work Handoff Failures (Medium/Medium)
**Symptoms**: Context lost when rotating agents, work duplicated or dropped.
**Mitigations**:
- First-Class Session Handoff Protocol
- Structured context transfer with acknowledgment
- Resource handover (reservations, locks)
- Pending work queue transfer

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Handoff preserves all context
- [ ] Reservations transfer to new agent
- [ ] Pending tasks not lost
- [ ] Handoff audit trail complete

### 6. Checkpoint Storage Explosion (Medium/Medium)
**Symptoms**: Disk fills up with checkpoint data.
**Mitigations**:
- Delta-based progressive checkpointing (~55% reduction)
- Automatic compaction of old chains
- Configurable retention policies
- Storage tiering (hot -> cold)

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Delta checkpoints are <20% of full
- [ ] Compaction runs on schedule
- [ ] Retention deletes old checkpoints
- [ ] Storage alarms trigger before full

### 7. Network Interruption (Medium/Medium)
**Symptoms**: WebSocket disconnects, output lost.
**Mitigations**:
- Durable ring buffers preserve messages
- Cursor-based reconnection with replay
- Offline queue for pending messages
- Heartbeat with auto-reconnect

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Reconnection within 5 seconds
- [ ] No messages lost during disconnect
- [ ] Cursor resumes from correct position
- [ ] Offline queue drains on reconnect

### 8. Daemon Failure (Low/High)
**Symptoms**: Gateway service crashes, all agents orphaned.
**Mitigations**:
- Supervisor with auto-restart
- Health checks every 30 seconds
- Alert on repeated crashes
- Graceful agent handover on planned shutdown

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Service restarts within 5 seconds
- [ ] Alert after 3 crashes in 5 minutes
- [ ] Agents survive daemon restart
- [ ] Planned shutdown preserves state

### 9. Data Loss (Low/Critical)
**Symptoms**: Checkpoints, history, or bead data permanently lost.
**Mitigations**:
- WAL mode for SQLite durability
- Delta checkpoints with redundancy
- Git coordination backs up state
- Regular backup verification

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Kill -9 doesn't lose data
- [ ] Checkpoint restoration verified
- [ ] Git contains all bead state
- [ ] Backup restore tested quarterly

### 10. Security Breach (Low/Critical)
**Symptoms**: Unauthorized access, data exfiltration.
**Mitigations**:
- BYOA token isolation (workspace-local)
- API key encryption at rest
- Audit logging of all access
- Safety guardrails (SLB, DCG)

**Testing Requirements**:
- [ ] Tokens never leave workspace
- [ ] Keys encrypted in database
- [ ] Audit captures all access
- [ ] DCG blocks dangerous commands

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 16 risks have assigned mitigation beads
- [ ] Each risk has documented testing requirements
- [ ] Mitigation effectiveness can be measured
- [ ] Residual risk level documented
- [ ] Runbook exists for each high-impact risk
- [ ] Alerts configured for early warning
- [ ] Quarterly risk review scheduled

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Risk detection logic works for each scenario
- [ ] Mitigation triggers activate at correct thresholds
- [ ] Fallback paths execute correctly

### Integration Tests
- [ ] End-to-end risk scenarios exercise mitigations
- [ ] Cross-component mitigations coordinate correctly
- [ ] Alert pipeline delivers notifications

### Chaos Tests
- [ ] Network partition handling
- [ ] Service crash recovery
- [ ] Storage exhaustion handling
- [ ] Provider outage simulation


### E2E Tests
- [ ] Scenario-based E2E suite (Playwright): inject representative failures (provider outage, DB down, WS reconnect) and verify UI shows actionable recovery steps.
- [ ] Alerting E2E: when a mitigation triggers, an alert is emitted and visible in the UI notification center.
- [ ] Artifact capture: trace/video/screenshot/console logs captured on failure and scrubbed of secrets.

### Logging
- [ ] Risk/chaos tests log a run-level correlationId, injected fault, expected mitigation, observed result, and any generated alert IDs (no secrets).
## References

-{"id":"flywheel_gateway-lil","title":"TASK: Parity Gate Tests","description":"## Background

Parity Gate Tests ensure that the Flywheel Gateway maintains consistency across all its interfaces and documentation. As the system grows with multiple access methods (REST API, WebSocket, CLI, AI-assisted), it becomes critical to verify that:

1. Every command is accessible through all intended interfaces
2. Documentation stays synchronized with implementation
3. Schema definitions match actual behavior
4. No commands are accidentally "orphaned" without proper bindings

### The Parity Problem

Without automated verification, systems tend to drift:
- New commands added without REST bindings
- AI hints outdated or missing for commands
- OpenAPI spec doesn't match actual endpoints
- WebSocket events undocumented
- Schema validation inconsistent

Parity Gate Tests run in CI to catch these issues before merge.

## Technical Architecture

### Test Categories

```typescript
// scripts/parity-gate.test.ts

describe('Command Registry Parity', () => {
  // Category 1: REST Binding Coverage
  describe('REST Bindings', () => {
    it('every registered command has a REST endpoint');
    it('REST endpoints match command signatures');
    it('HTTP methods are appropriate for command type');
    it('route parameters match command inputs');
  });
  
  // Category 2: AI Hint Coverage
  describe('AI Hints', () => {
    it('every command has AI hints defined');
    it('AI hints include description');
    it('AI hints include example usage');
    it('AI hints include parameter explanations');
    it('AI hints are under 500 tokens');
  });
  
  // Category 3: Schema Coverage
  describe('Input/Output Schemas', () => {
    it('every command has input schema');
    it('every command has output schema');
    it('schemas are valid JSON Schema draft-07');
    it('required fields are marked');
    it('examples validate against schema');
  });
  
  // Category 4: OpenAPI Consistency
  describe('OpenAPI Spec', () => {
    it('all REST endpoints are in OpenAPI spec');
    it('OpenAPI schemas match TypeScript types');
    it('response codes are documented');
    it('error responses are standardized');
    it('OpenAPI examples are valid');
  });
  
  // Category 5: WebSocket Documentation
  describe('WebSocket Events', () => {
    it('all events are documented');
    it('event payloads have schemas');
    it('subscription patterns are documented');
    it('error events are standardized');
  });
});
```

### Command Registry Scanner

```typescript
// scripts/lib/registry-scanner.ts
interface RegisteredCommand {

  handler: string;

}
```

## Test Implementation

```typescript
// scripts/parity-gate.test.ts
import { describe, it, expect, beforeAll } from 'vitest';
import { RegistryScanner } from './lib/registry-scanner';
import { OpenAPIValidator } from './lib/openapi-validator';
import { WebSocketDocChecker } from './lib/websocket-doc-checker';

describe('Parity Gate Tests', () => {
  let commands: RegisteredCommand[];
  let restBindings: RestBinding[];
  let openAPISpec: OpenAPIDocument;
  
  beforeAll(async () => {
    const scanner = new RegistryScanner();
    commands = await scanner.scanCommands();
    restBindings = await scanner.scanRestControllers();
    openAPISpec = await new OpenAPIValidator().loadSpec('./docs/openapi.yaml');
  });
  
  describe('REST Binding Coverage', () => {
    it('every registered command has a REST endpoint', () => {
      const commandNames = commands.map(c => c.name);
      const boundCommands = restBindings.map(b => b.commandName);
      
      const unbound = commandNames.filter(n => !boundCommands.includes(n));
      
      expect(unbound).toEqual([]);
      expect(unbound.length).toBe(0);
    });
    
    it('REST methods match command semantics', () => {
      for (const binding of restBindings) {
        const command = commands.find(c => c.name === binding.commandName);
        
        if (command.name.startsWith('get') || command.name.startsWith('list')) {
          expect(binding.method).toBe('GET');
        }
        if (command.name.startsWith('create')) {
          expect(binding.method).toBe('POST');
        }
        if (command.name.startsWith('update')) {
          expect(['PUT', 'PATCH']).toContain(binding.method);
        }
        if (command.name.startsWith('delete')) {
          expect(binding.method).toBe('DELETE');
        }
      }
    });
  });
  
  describe('AI Hint Coverage', () => {
    it('every command has AI hints', () => {
      const missingHints = commands.filter(c => !c.aiHints);
      
      expect(missingHints.map(c => c.name)).toEqual([]);
    });
    
    it('AI hints have required fields', () => {
      for (const command of commands) {
        if (command.aiHints) {
          expect(command.aiHints.description).toBeTruthy();
          expect(command.aiHints.description.length).toBeGreaterThan(10);
          expect(command.aiHints.examples.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
        }
      }
    });
    
    it('AI hints are concise (under 500 tokens)', () => {
      for (const command of commands) {
        if (command.aiHints) {
          expect(command.aiHints.tokenCount).toBeLessThan(500);
        }
      }
    });
  });
  
  describe('Schema Coverage', () => {
    it('every command has input schema', () => {
      const missingInput = commands.filter(c => !c.inputSchema);
      expect(missingInput.map(c => c.name)).toEqual([]);
    });
    
    it('every command has output schema', () => {
      const missingOutput = commands.filter(c => !c.outputSchema);
      expect(missingOutput.map(c => c.name)).toEqual([]);
    });
    
    it('schemas are valid JSON Schema', async () => {
      const Ajv = await import('ajv');
      const ajv = new Ajv.default();
      
      for (const command of commands) {
        if (command.inputSchema) {
          expect(() => ajv.compile(command.inputSchema)).not.toThrow();
        }
        if (command.outputSchema) {
          expect(() => ajv.compile(command.outputSchema)).not.toThrow();
        }
      }
    });
  });
  
  describe('OpenAPI Consistency', () => {
    it('all REST endpoints are in OpenAPI spec', async () => {
      const validator = new OpenAPIValidator();
      const result = await validator.validateAgainstRoutes(openAPISpec, restBindings);
      
      expect(result.coverage.percentage).toBe(100);
      expect(result.errors).toEqual([]);
    });
    
    it('OpenAPI response codes are documented', () => {
      for (const path of Object.values(openAPISpec.paths)) {
        for (const operation of Object.values(path)) {
          expect(operation.responses['200'] || operation.responses['201']).toBeTruthy();
          expect(operation.responses['400']).toBeTruthy();
          expect(operation.responses['500']).toBeTruthy();
        }
      }
    });
  });
  
  describe('WebSocket Event Documentation', () => {
    it('all events are documented', async () => {
      const checker = new WebSocketDocChecker();
      const undocumented = await checker.findUndocumentedEvents();
      
      expect(undocumented).toEqual([]);
    });
  });
});
```

## CI Integration

```yaml
# .github/workflows/parity-gate.yml
name: Parity Gate

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  parity-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
        
      - name: Run Parity Gate Tests
        run: npm run test:parity
        
      - name: Upload Coverage Report
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: parity-report
          path: scripts/reports/
```

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-ls4","title":"FEAT: Shared Error Taxonomy + AI Hints","description":"## Background

Flywheel Gateway requires a unified error handling system that serves three distinct audiences:
1. **Human Developers** - Need clear, actionable error messages during development
2. **AI Agents** - Need structured hints to understand what went wrong and how to recover
3. **Client Applications** - Need consistent HTTP status codes and error formats for programmatic handling

The error taxonomy must be comprehensive enough to cover all failure modes across the gateway ecosystem while remaining extensible for future use cases.

## Technical Rationale

### Why a Shared Error Package?
- **Consistency**: All packages (gateway, drivers, SDK) use identical error codes
- **Type Safety**: TypeScript discriminated unions ensure exhaustive error handling
- **AI Integration**: Built-in hints help AI agents self-correct without human intervention
- **Observability**: Structured errors enable better logging, metrics, and alerting

### Error Code Design Philosophy
Error codes follow the pattern: `{DOMAIN}_{SPECIFIC_ERROR}`
- Domain prefixes: `AGENT_`, `SPAWN_`, `DRIVER_`, `WS_`, `AUTH_`, `RATE_`, `SYSTEM_`
- This enables filtering/routing by domain while maintaining specificity

## Scope & Requirements

### Core Error Codes to Define

**Agent Lifecycle Errors:**
- `AGENT_NOT_FOUND` - Agent ID does not exist or was garbage collected
- `AGENT_ALREADY_EXISTS` - Attempt to spawn with duplicate ID
- `AGENT_TERMINATED` - Operation on already-terminated agent
- `AGENT_BUSY` - Agent currently processing, cannot accept new commands
- `AGENT_TIMEOUT` - Agent did not respond within configured timeout

**Spawn Errors:**
- `SPAWN_FAILED` - Generic spawn failure (driver-specific details in metadata)
- `SPAWN_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` - User/workspace has reached agent spawn limit
- `SPAWN_INVALID_CONFIG` - Invalid spawn configuration parameters
- `SPAWN_DRIVER_UNAVAILABLE` - Requested driver not available/healthy

**Driver Errors:**
- `DRIVER_NOT_FOUND` - Unknown driver type requested
- `DRIVER_INIT_FAILED` - Driver failed to initialize
- `DRIVER_COMMUNICATION_ERROR` - Lost connection to underlying agent process
- `DRIVER_PROTOCOL_ERROR` - Unexpected message format from agent

**WebSocket Errors:**
- `WS_CONNECTION_FAILED` - Failed to establish WebSocket connection
- `WS_AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED` - Missing or invalid auth token
- `WS_SUBSCRIPTION_DENIED` - Not authorized for requested channel
- `WS_CURSOR_EXPIRED` - Requested replay cursor no longer in ring buffer
- `WS_RATE_LIMITED` - Too many messages/connections

**Authentication/Authorization:**
- `AUTH_TOKEN_INVALID` - Malformed or expired token
- `AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRED` - Token past expiration time
- `AUTH_INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE` - Token lacks required permissions
- `AUTH_workspace_SUSPENDED` - workspace account suspended

**Rate Limiting:**
- `RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` - Request rate limit exceeded
- `RATE_CONCURRENT_LIMIT` - Too many concurrent operations

**System Errors:**
- `SYSTEM_UNAVAILABLE` - Service temporarily unavailable
- `SYSTEM_INTERNAL_ERROR` - Unexpected internal error
- `SYSTEM_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED` - Memory/CPU/disk limits reached

### HTTP Status Mappings

```typescript

```

### AI-Friendly Error Structure

```typescript
interface GatewayError {
  code: ErrorCode;
  message: string;           // Human-readable message
  httpStatus: number;
  
  // AI Hints - structured guidance for agent recovery
  aiHint: {
    severity: 'recoverable' | 'terminal' | 'retry';
    suggestedAction: string;

    alternativeApproach?: string;
  };

}
```

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-mag","title":"Performance Optimization","description":"## Background

As the Flywheel Gateway scales to handle more agents, sessions, and real-time events, performance becomes critical. Users expect instantaneous UI responses, smooth animations, and efficient resource usage. This bead addresses performance at multiple levels: network (WebSocket), rendering (virtualization), bundle size, and computation offloading.

## Reasoning

Performance directly impacts user experience and system reliability:

1. **WebSocket Backpressure**: Prevents memory exhaustion when server sends data faster than client can process
2. **Virtualization**: Enables displaying thousands of log lines without DOM bloat
3. **Bundle Optimization**: Faster initial load times, especially on mobile networks
4. **Web Workers**: Keeps UI responsive during heavy computations
5. **CI Budgets**: Prevents performance regressions from reaching production

## Technical Considerations

### WebSocket Backpressure Handling

```typescript
// apps/web/src/lib/websocket/BackpressureManager.ts
interface BackpressureConfig {
  highWaterMark: number;    // Start applying backpressure (default: 1000 messages)
  lowWaterMark: number;     // Resume normal flow (default: 100 messages)
  maxQueueSize: number;     // Drop oldest messages if exceeded (default: 5000)
  processingInterval: number; // Batch processing interval (default: 16ms)
}

class BackpressureManager {
  private queue: Message[] = [];
  private isPaused = false;
  
  enqueue(message: Message): void {
    if (this.queue.length >= this.config.maxQueueSize) {
      // Drop oldest messages, keep newest
      this.queue = this.queue.slice(-this.config.lowWaterMark);
      console.warn('WebSocket queue overflow, dropped old messages');
    }
    
    this.queue.push(message);
    
    if (this.queue.length >= this.config.highWaterMark && !this.isPaused) {
      this.pause();
    }
  }
  
  processQueue(): Message[] {
    const batch = this.queue.splice(0, 100); // Process 100 at a time
    
    if (this.queue.length <= this.config.lowWaterMark && this.isPaused) {
      this.resume();
    }
    
    return batch;
  }
}
```

### Output Virtualization with react-window

```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/VirtualizedOutput.tsx
import { VariableSizeList } from 'react-window';
import AutoSizer from 'react-virtualized-auto-sizer';

interface OutputLine {
  id: string;
  content: string;
  timestamp: number;
  type: 'stdout' | 'stderr' | 'system';
}

function VirtualizedOutput({ lines }: { lines: OutputLine[] }) {
  const listRef = useRef<VariableSizeList>(null);
  const rowHeights = useRef<Map<number, number>>(new Map());
  
  // Dynamic row heights for wrapped text
  const getRowHeight = (index: number) => {
    return rowHeights.current.get(index) || 24; // Default single line height
  };
  
  const setRowHeight = (index: number, height: number) => {
    if (rowHeights.current.get(index) !== height) {
      rowHeights.current.set(index, height);
      listRef.current?.resetAfterIndex(index);
    }
  };
  
  return (
    <AutoSizer>
      {({ height, width }) => (
        <VariableSizeList
          ref={listRef}
          height={height}
          width={width}
          itemCount={lines.length}
          itemSize={getRowHeight}
          overscanCount={20}
        >
          {({ index, style }) => (
            <OutputRow 
              line={lines[index]} 
              style={style}
              onHeightChange={(h) => setRowHeight(index, h)}
            />
          )}
        </VariableSizeList>
      )}
    </AutoSizer>
  );
}
```

```typescript
// apps/web/src/components/OptimizedImage.tsx
interface OptimizedImageProps {
  src: string;
  alt: string;
  width: number;
  height: number;
  priority?: boolean;
}

function OptimizedImage({ src, alt, width, height, priority = false }: OptimizedImageProps) {
  // Generate srcset for responsive images
  const srcSet = [1, 2, 3].map(scale => 
    `${src}?w=${width * scale}&format=webp ${scale}x`
  ).join(', ');
  
  return (
    <picture>
      <source srcSet={srcSet} type="image/webp" />
      <img
        src={src}
        alt={alt}
        width={width}
        height={height}
        loading={priority ? 'eager' : 'lazy'}
        decoding={priority ? 'sync' : 'async'}
      />
    </picture>
  );
}
```

### Web Workers for Heavy Computation

```typescript
// apps/web/src/workers/logParser.worker.ts
import { ParsedLog, RawLog } from '@/types/logs';

self.onmessage = (event: MessageEvent<RawLog[]>) => {
  const rawLogs = event.data;
  
  // Heavy parsing/filtering that would block main thread
  const parsed: ParsedLog[] = rawLogs.map(log => ({
    ...log,
    parsed: parseAnsiCodes(log.content),
    searchableText: stripAnsi(log.content).toLowerCase(),
    timestamp: new Date(log.timestamp),
    level: detectLogLevel(log.content)
  }));
  
  self.postMessage(parsed);
};

// apps/web/src/hooks/useLogParser.ts
export function useLogParser() {
  const workerRef = useRef<Worker | null>(null);
  
  useEffect(() => {
    workerRef.current = new Worker(
      new URL('../workers/logParser.worker.ts', import.meta.url),
      { type: 'module' }
    );
    
    return () => workerRef.current?.terminate();
  }, []);
  
  const parseLogs = useCallback((logs: RawLog[]): Promise<ParsedLog[]> => {
    return new Promise((resolve) => {
      if (!workerRef.current) {
        // Fallback to main thread if worker unavailable
        resolve(parseLogsSync(logs));
        return;
      }
      
      workerRef.current.onmessage = (e) => resolve(e.data);
      workerRef.current.postMessage(logs);
    });
  }, []);
  
  return { parseLogs };
}
```

 {"id":"flywheel_gateway-msz","title":"Conflict Detection and Alerts","description":"## Background

Conflict Detection and Alerts provides proactive identification of potential conflicts before they cause problems. While the File Reservation System prevents conflicts through coordination, this system detects conflicts that occur despite reservations or in uncoordinated scenarios.

Three types of conflicts are detected:
1. **Reservation Overlaps**: Two agents request conflicting file patterns
2. **Git Conflicts**: Actual merge conflicts in version control
3. **Resource Contention**: Competition for shared resources (APIs, databases)

## Technical Architecture

### Conflict Types
```typescript
type ConflictType = 
  | 'reservation_overlap'    // Pattern intersection
  | 'git_merge_conflict'     // Actual file conflict
  | 'git_potential_conflict' // Same file, different branches
  | 'resource_contention'    // API rate limits, DB locks
  | 'deadlock_detected';     // Circular wait condition

interface Conflict {
  id: string;
  type: ConflictType;
  severity: 'info' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'critical';
  projectId: string;
  involvedAgents: string[];
  affectedResources: string[];  // Files, APIs, etc.
  detectedAt: Date;
  resolvedAt?: Date;
  resolution?: ConflictResolution;
  metadata: Record<string, any>;
}
```

### Detection Strategies

#### 1. Reservation Overlap Detection
Real-time detection when reservations are created:
```typescript
async detectReservationOverlap(
  newReservation: ReservationRequest
): Promise<OverlapConflict[]> {
  const activeReservations = await this.getActiveReservations(
    newReservation.projectId
  );
  
  return activeReservations
    .filter(r => r.agentId !== newReservation.agentId)
    .map(r => ({
      existing: r,
      overlap: this.computeOverlap(r.patterns, newReservation.patterns)
    }))
    .filter(r => r.overlap.length > 0);
}
```

#### 2. Git Conflict Detection
Periodic and event-driven git status checking:
```typescript
async detectGitConflicts(projectId: string): Promise<GitConflict[]> {
  const conflicts: GitConflict[] = [];
  
  // Check for actual merge conflicts
  const mergeStatus = await this.git.status();
  if (mergeStatus.conflicted.length > 0) {
    conflicts.push({
      type: 'git_merge_conflict',
      files: mergeStatus.conflicted,
      severity: 'critical'
    });
  }
  
  // Check for potential conflicts (same file modified on different branches)
  const branches = await this.getActiveBranches(projectId);
  for (const [b1, b2] of combinations(branches, 2)) {
    const commonFiles = await this.findCommonModifiedFiles(b1, b2);
    if (commonFiles.length > 0) {
      conflicts.push({
        type: 'git_potential_conflict',
        files: commonFiles,
        branches: [b1, b2],
        severity: 'warning'
      });
    }
  }
  
  return conflicts;
}
```

#### 3. Resource Contention Detection
Monitor shared resource access patterns:
```typescript
interface ResourceAccess {
  resourceId: string;
  agentId: string;
  accessType: 'read' | 'write' | 'exclusive';
  timestamp: Date;
}

async detectResourceContention(
  projectId: string,
  windowMs: number = 5000
): Promise<ContentionConflict[]> {
  const recentAccesses = await this.getRecentAccesses(projectId, windowMs);
  
  // Group by resource
  const byResource = groupBy(recentAccesses, 'resourceId');
  
  return Object.entries(byResource)
    .filter(([_, accesses]) => this.hasContention(accesses))
    .map(([resourceId, accesses]) => ({
      type: 'resource_contention',
      resourceId,
      involvedAgents: unique(accesses.map(a => a.agentId)),
      severity: 'warning'
    }));
}
```

### Recommended Actions
System suggests resolutions based on conflict type:
```typescript
function getRecommendedActions(conflict: Conflict): Action[] {
  switch (conflict.type) {
    case 'reservation_overlap':
      return [
        { action: 'wait', description: 'Wait for existing reservation to expire' },
        { action: 'negotiate', description: 'Request partial pattern release' },
        { action: 'force', description: 'Override (requires admin)' }
      ];
    
    case 'git_merge_conflict':
      return [
        { action: 'manual_resolve', description: 'Human intervention required' },
        { action: 'abort_merge', description: 'Abort and retry later' },
        { action: 'accept_ours', description: 'Keep current branch changes' },
        { action: 'accept_theirs', description: 'Accept incoming changes' }
      ];
    
    case 'resource_contention':
      return [
        { action: 'queue', description: 'Queue request for later' },
        { action: 'retry_backoff', description: 'Retry with exponential backoff' },
        { action: 'alternative', description: 'Use alternative resource' }
      ];
    
    default:
      return [{ action: 'investigate', description: 'Manual investigation needed' }];
  }
}
```
